programme.desc: The TV version of the popular radio show taking a light-hearted view of all things related to Brexit and beyond, with Laura Kuenssberg, Katya Adler, Adam Fleming and Chris Mason engaging in banter about the ongoing process to leave the EU(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Jon Kay and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Crimewatch Roadshow
programme.desc: Michelle Ackerley heads to south Wales to discover how police forces bring different teams together to train for the most challenging situations. Back in the studio, Rav Wilding has the latest crime appeals from all over the UK, as well as tips, advice and features(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell explore a couple of terraces in Liverpool and Alfreton in Derbyshire, as well as a rambling former pub in Rowelton, Cumbria(n)

programme.title: Defenders UK
programme.desc: The enforcement officers battle to keep the roads safe after a lorry driver killed four people as a result of using his mobile phone while driving. They also crack down on the rogue airport car parking companies ripping off holiday-makers(n)

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: Nick Knowles examines the extraordinary rescue of a young man missing overnight after being swept out to sea on his surfboard. Plus, a local resident comes to the aid of a young woman who topples into a city-centre river during a night on the town with friends(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Eric Knowles hosts as experts Nick Hall and Richard Madley help two teams scour Exeter's Westpoint Arena for items. The presenter also receives a visit by the chairman of the Torquay Pottery Collectors Society, who has a rather special pasty to show him(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Al has bonded with Ray, but Eve is about to discover something to upset them all. Sid offers support to a friend of his father who is struggling with getting older(n)

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Seventeen contenders start this edition of the quiz, but how many of them will survive to face the £10,000 Question? Hosted by Rick Edwards(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich are tasked to create a garden that fuses Japan and Yorkshire - in Cheshunt, Hertfordshire. Yorkshire-born James wants to see hints of his native county in his new Home Counties house, while his Japanese wife Akika, who he met when traveling, naturally wants to feel a link to her upbringing and homeland(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Arusha Irvine enjoys the fun of the fair, while Angus Ashworth heads to Plymouth to discover the turbulent history of gin, before having a go as a Master Distiller. They then join up at their final auction in Clevedon, north Somerset(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: Strictly: It Takes Two
programme.desc: Zoe Ball and Rylan Clark-Neal present analysis of the pro-celebrity couples' progress ahead of tomorrow night's show, and reports from the rehearsals. Celebrity super fans discuss all things ballroom, while Gethin Jones catches up with the couples backstage at this week's Strictly rehearsals(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: The usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and green-fingered siblings Harry and David Rich travel to Milton Keynes to compete for the chance to create a garden for husband and wife Vinay and Raunak. Vinay loves to host barbeques, so he's after a garden where he can do all that in style. Raunak, meanwhile, is more interested in filling the garden with vivid and dramatic colours, to match the style of decor she has used throughout their house. Both want the garden to represent the Hindu religion's belief in the power and importance of nature(n)

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: A World Athletics Championships edition of the quiz, which sees former Great Britain stars Denise Lewis, Liz McColgan, Greg Rutherford and Kriss Akabusi join host Sue Barker and team captains Phil Tufnell and Matt Dawson on the quiz(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Callum shares his concerns about Rainie to Stuart but his mind turns elsewhere when Whitney returns to collect the rest of her belongings - and is taken aback when she drop a bombshell. Sharon is left fearing the worst, and Ben asks an old friend for help with his new money-making scheme(n)

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: Contenders battle for the remaining places in the semi-finals, tasked to cook a faultless two-course meal that will not only be judged by John Torode and Gregg Wallace but also by former contenders Alexis Conran, Sophie Thompson and the Rev Richard Coles. All three know what it takes to get through this competition and are in for a treat as the four celebs push the boat out to extend their stay in the contest(n)

programme.title: Scarborough
programme.desc: After a bit of gentle persuasion by Marion, Mike and Karen decide to move in together and go flat-hunting in the town. However, Mike's promise to Karen that he'll tell Bigsy that he's moving out is proving more difficult to deliver than he thought. Bigsy has other things on his mind though, namely Lisa Marie. Meanwhile, Geraldine finally admits to Karen that she's feeling the strain of the salon's financial difficulties and things only get worse when the police pay her a visit. Comedy, starring Maggie Ollerenshaw, Catherine Tyldesley and Jason Manford(n)

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host greets Oscar-winning actress Helen Mirren, who is starring in the new TV drama Catherine the Great. Author, adventurer and documentary-maker Simon Reeve also appears, as does comedian and actor Jack Whitehall, who stood in as host for Graham during the spring. Plus, the one and only Rupaul discusses bringing his hit show to the BBC in RuPaul's Drag Race UK. Music is supplied courtesy of Normani, who performs her new single Motivation(n)

programme.title: Lewis Capaldi Live
programme.desc: The breakthrough pop sensation performs the biggest headline show of his career at Croxteth Park in Liverpool, entertaining a sell-out crowd of 12,500 people. Accompanied by the 60-piece Manchester Camerata, Lewis delights the audience with hits from his platinum-selling debut album and a special surprise cover of a Beatles classic(n)

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Back in 1983, 16-year-old Amy Taskerland was murdered on the night of the school disco. More than 30 years later, the unearthing of a time capsule and the discovery of her voice on a mix tape inside prompts Miller and co to delve back into the case, starting by interviewing Amy's best friend Harriet and her former teacher Mr Hines. The recording features a strange mix of music and poetry, including words from a speech written in secret for the Queen for broadcast in the event of a nuclear war. But how did Amy get a copy of it, and was its secret nature the reason she was killed? Elsewhere, Miller enjoys her first romance since her divorce, while Gerry goes on his future son-in-law's stag night. Amy Nuttall (Emmerdale, Downton Abbey) and Jack Shepherd (Wycliffe) guest star(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Jon Kay and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45(n)

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Ching-He Huang and Cyrus Todiwala, and special guest Alexander Armstrong. Plus, archive clips featuring Rick Stein, Keith Floyd, and Nigella Lawson(n)

programme.title: Mary Berry's Quick Cooking
programme.desc: Mary heads into London's West End for inspiration, seeking out the secrets of getting busy people fed quickly and well. She learns the art of high-speed wok cooking in Chinatown and sampling the sweet treats being made daily at London's first-ever patisserie, before seeing a show and having a late-night cocktail with some of the cast members. Back in her own kitchen, Mary creates an Asian beef and pepper stir fry, a chocolate cappuccino pie and an easy, speedy breakfast for those on the go(n)

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests look ahead to the weekend's football, including Everton v Manchester City. Plus, updates on the lunchtime game between Sheffield United and Liverpool(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion Dublin visits a bungalow in Cheshire and an old chapel in Dudley ripe for development into flats. Martel Maxwell is in West Yorkshire, where the new owners of an end of terrace have grand plans for a child-friendly development(n)

programme.title: Live Athletics: World Championships
programme.desc: Gabby Logan presents coverage of day two of the biennial event, held this year at Khalifa International Stadium in Doha, Qatar. Great Britain's Dina Asher-Smith begins her bid for a sprint double with the heats of the 100m, while other events include the heats of the men's 800m and qualifying for the men's discus and pole vault. With analysis from Michael Johnson(n)

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents a round-up of the classified results and this afternoon's news from the Premier League, EFL and Scotland(n)

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman invite another selection of famous faces to take part in the unorthodox general knowledge quiz. As always, the challengers are tasked with coming up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public. This time, all the contestants come from the world of music, as Tom Ogden, Ray BLK, Shaun Ryder, Rowetta, Ricky Wilson, Will Young, Naughty Boy and Lesley Garrett take part. Plus, a special performance from the band Blossoms(n)

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman present the first live episode of this year's series, with a line-up featuring the likes of Alex Scott, Emma Barton and James Cracknell. The celebrities are dancing to impress the four judges and the viewers at home, who get the chance to vote for their favourites for the very first time this series(n)

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Hospital drama with the staff of Holby's accident and emergency department as they deal with more sick and injured patients. David and Ollie continue to clash after Ollie's operation, and David is taken aback when he finds out that Ollie has invited Rosa round to keep him company. Meanwhile, Rash decides to fight to stay in Holby, and new paramedic Lev arrives late for work after saving some kittens. Starring Jason Durr, Harry Collett, Jacey Salles, Neet Mohan and Uriel Emil(n)

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: Cathy seems reluctant to reveal any information about her new boyfriend, Mark refuses to talk about why his wife has thrown him out, and Dermot lies to his fiancee's parents about his job - all of which makes Agnes feel her family is becoming too secretive. Comedy, written by and starring Brendan O'Carroll(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Sheffield United v Liverpool at Bramall Lane and Everton v Manchester City at Goodison Park, as both of last season's top two faced potentially difficult away games. Plus, Aston Villa v Burnley, Bournemouth v West Ham United, Chelsea v Brighton & Hove Albion, Crystal Palace v Norwich City, Tottenham Hotspur v Southampton and Wolverhampton Wanderers v Watford(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Roo Irvine and Angus Ashworth continue to scour the shops of south Wales for antiques, where one expert takes a punt on a piece of local history(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich create a Boy Scouts-inspired garden, complete with campfire and winding path, for Thomas and Kate in Harborne, Birmingham(n)

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team are at Dover Castle for a special edition first aired to mark 80 years since the start of the Second World War, bringing insights into the conflict through personal stories and family mementos. A photo album belonging to an Austrian soldier offers a previously unseen glimpse of Neville Chamberlain's ill-fated meeting with Adolf Hitler in 1938, while Fiona Bruce meets 95-year-old Ray Palmer, who has the rare distinction of being both a child evacuee and then a serving soldier(n)

programme.title: Victoria Derbyshire
programme.desc: Daily news and current-affairs programme offering discussion of breaking stories, exclusive interviews and audience interaction via social media(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsroom Live
programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Andrew Neil and guests discuss the big political issues of the day(n)

programme.title: Hardball
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Ore Oduba in which contestants have to answer questions to get as far along a track as they can before a ball catches and passes them, ending their go(n)

programme.title: Home Away from Home
programme.desc: Two couples from Taunton and the Peak District test drive a new way of life as they swap homes with each other. Presented by Gyles Brandreth(n)

programme.title: Heir Hunters
programme.desc: The search for a Kent woman's relatives runs into difficulties on her father's side of the family. The team uncovers the story of a naval hero during a case in Wales(n)

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Alice Roberts spends a year visiting archaeological digs around the country, finding out about discoveries that shed new light on British history during the prehistoric, Roman, Saxon and Tudor eras(n)

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Food writer Mary Berry is one of Britain's best-loved TV cooks, finding fame thanks to her judging role on The Great British Bake Off. A self-confessed go-getter, she wants to trace her family tree to know where she gets her energy and drive from. The journey begins in Bath, where Mary grew up, and takes her to Norwich, where both her grandparents lived. Along the way she uncovers ancestors including a bankrupt book-binder, a self-motivated corset-maker and a third who worked in a much more familiar profession(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Featuring previously unseen finds from the show's travels around Britain, visiting sites including Muncaster Castle in Cumbria and the Bowes Museum in County Durham(n)

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize(n)

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo travels between Liverpool and Wales. Along the way he discovers a childhood favourite - a miniature tank engine with three carriages in dark red - made by Frank Hornby, and learns the story of a Welsh countess who lived in Gwrych Castle. In Snowdonia, he also rides the waves with a Welsh surfing champion(n)

programme.title: MOTDx
programme.desc: Jermaine Jenas and guests Craig Mitch, Chelcee Grimes and Reece Parkinson talk about the Premier League and everything that is loved about it - the skills, celebs, music, fashion and culture(n)

programme.title: Jessica Ennis-Hill's World Class of 2019
programme.desc: The former athlete looks ahead to the 17th staging of the World Athletics Championships, which this year take place in Doha, Qatar. The presenter, a three-time gold medallist in these games herself, talks to British contenders Dina Asher-Smith and Katarina Johnson-Thompson, and Norwegian middle-distance brothers Henrik and Jakob Ingebrigtsen(n)

programme.title: Hairy Bikers: Route 66
programme.desc: The third leg of Dave Myers and Si King's trip sees them on the trail of Oklahoma's famous beef. In Oklahoma City, a classic T-bone steak is followed by a visit to the city's Vietnamese community, where they knock up their national dish pho - a broth made of beef or chicken. After checking out El Reno's unique onion burger the duo hook up with a couple of fire crews who like to cook - especially brisket. An organic sweet potato farm inspires Dave and Si to make sweet potato brownies, before they finish this stretch on a cattle ranch with America's most iconic custodian of the land - the cowboy(n)

programme.title: The Conspiracy Files
programme.desc: As Britain loses its measles-free status and cases surge worldwide, the World Health Organisation has labelled 'vaccine hesitancy' a top 10 threat to global health. But campaigners claim that vaccines are unsafe, and the cause of a health catastrophe. Decades of scientific research do not back them up. Yet their ideas not only persist - they are reaching around the globe. This documentary examines the arguments of those who campaign against vaccines, and hears from people at the heart of alleged conspiracies. And it analyses how vaccine-sceptic ideas are spreading, what gives them currency and who may be benefiting(n)

programme.title: The Mash Report
programme.desc: Nish Kumar and a cast of comedy correspondents including Ellie Taylor and Steve N Allen present the satirical and surreal take on the week's news(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Mark Urban(n)

programme.title: Interior Design Masters
programme.desc: As the semi-finals get underway, the remaining three contestants go head to head for the first time as they are challenged to redesign an entire hair salon, with industry experts Kelly Hoppen, Sophie Robinson and Abigail Ahern on hand to offer the designers their advice. With just two days to complete this ambitious challenge, the three designers head to sunny Whitstable on the Kent coast to visit the salons for the first time. One designer decides to go for an ambitious theme with sophisticated lighting scheme, while another opts for bold colours and brave design choices. In the third salon, the competitor decides to play it safe, but will their salon have enough ambition to impress the judges and earn a place in the final? Judging the finished salons alongside Michelle Ogundehin this week is design legend, Lawrence Llewelyn- Bowen(n)

programme.title: The $50m Art Swindle
programme.desc: Documentary telling the remarkable story of a charlatan art dealer who swindled more than $50million from the art establishment before going on the run. Michel Cohen, a popular and charming New York art dealer originally from France, was a high-school drop-out from a poor background who went on to become a rich and successful art dealer. Throughout the 1990s, he sold paintings by artists such as Picasso, Monet and Chagall to America's wealthiest elite. Cohen was living the high life until he began trading recklessly in the stock market and ran up considerable debts. In an attempt to recoup his losses, he swindled private collectors, auction houses and other art dealers out of more than $50million. When his crimes were discovered, he fled the US with his wife and two children and went on the run. In 2003, he was found by Interpol in Brazil and was imprisoned in Rio de Janeiro, but seven months later while awaiting extradition to the US, he escaped from prison and vanished off the face o(n)

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Comedian Paul Merton investigates his family history. His mother's Irish parents died when she was very young, so Paul sets out to piece together the story of his grandfather's involvement in key events leading up to Irish independence in the 1920s, and learns that the true circumstances of his death were very different from the story that had been handed down. Elsewhere in London, Paul traces a great, great grandmother on his father's side of the family who was sent to prison for her part in an assault(n)

programme.title: Home Is Where the Art Is
programme.desc: Nick Knowles challenges three artists to create pieces for people they have never met, with the trio trying to work out the buyer's style and tastes by looking around their home, beginning by travelling to Lancashire to a renovated worker's cottage(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Arusha Irvine and Angus Ashworth tour the West Country, where Roo meets Exmoor's equine inhabitants and Angus bares all at an exclusive beach that can only be accessed by tunnel(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Alison and Janice love Australia and want a relaxing, Oz-inspired 'bolt hole', complete with cracked earth-beach look, water feature, palm trees and fire bowl in their Goring-by-Sea home, so the Rich Brothers and Charlie Dimmock head to West Sussex to see how they can help(n)

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: At Longmeadow, Monty Don adds some ornamental grasses to his borders for late summer impact and gives advice on how to look after pumpkins for a bountiful autumn harvest. Adam Frost discovers a colourful garden in Gloucestershire where hardy, tender and tropical plants jostle for space in both borders and containers, while Frances Tophill visits an allotment in north London where the main harvest is flowers for floral displays(n)

programme.title: Nadiya's Time to Eat
programme.desc: Nadiya Hussain shares the recipes she relies on when she hasn't got the energy to cook anything complicated, including French toast spiced with turmeric, falafel burgers, and crispy apple pies. Nadiya also heads to the Heinz factory in Wigan to find out how the team meet the public's demand for baked beans, while she joins a busy vet during lambing season to help him reinvigorate his lunchtimes(n)

programme.title: Victoria Derbyshire
programme.desc: Daily news and current-affairs programme offering discussion of breaking stories, exclusive interviews and audience interaction via social media(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsroom Live
programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Adam Fleming and guests discuss the big political issues of the day(n)

programme.title: Hardball
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Ore Oduba in which contestants have to answer questions to get as far along a track as they can before a ball catches and passes them, ending their go(n)

programme.title: Live Athletics: World Championships
programme.desc: Gabby Logan presents live coverage of day one of the biennial event, held this year at Khalifa International Stadium in Doha, Qatar. Heats take place on the track in the men's 100m, 5,000m and 400m hurdles, and women's 800m and 3,000m steeplechase. Meanwhile, in the field, qualifying takes place for the men's long jump and triple jump, and the women's hammer, pole vault and high jump. With analysis from Michael Johnson, Denise Lewis, Colin Jackson, Paula Radcliffe and Jessica Ennis-Hill(n)

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys grills four more contenders on their chosen subjects, which are the history of the Student Nonviolent Coordinating Committee, the life and career of football star Kenny Dalglish, the novels and novellas of Gabriel García Márquez and the films of writer-director Christopher Nolan. They are then each afforded two minutes to demonstrate their prowess on general knowledge(n)

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: A look at the role that plants and gardening play in people's mental and physical wellbeing, exploring the idea that gardening is a good and positive pastime. Arit Anderson travels to a medical centre in Kent where gardening is prescribed by doctors, to help patients cope with long-term mental and physical problems. Adam Frost is at a community garden in Oxfordshire, created to provide ongoing support for sufferers of Parkinson's disease. Back at Longmeadow, Monty Don plants bulbs for spring and catches up on some seasonal tasks(n)

programme.title: Inside the Vatican
programme.desc: The second of two programmes in which cameras record the inner workings of the Vatican over the course of a year. One of the most important tools of reform the Pope possesses is his power to appoint new cardinals. It's the closest he gets to succession planning, because the cardinals will elect the new Pope at the next conclave. Here, Pope Francis selects 14 new cardinals, breaking with tradition by choosing men outside the centres of power. As the pontiff prepares to make a first papal visit to Ireland, the Church is rocked by a report alleging the cover-up of sex abuses by priests, dating back decades(n)

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig looks at 'quacks' and 'quackery', with regular panellist Alan Davies and guest players Victoria Coren Mitchell, Stephen K Amos and Claudia Winkleman. As usual, the questions are so difficult points are instead awarded for the answers the host finds most interesting as opposed to correct(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Mark Urban(n)

programme.title: Front Row Late
programme.desc: As new exhibitions of the work of the renowned 18th-century satirical artist William Hogarth and the contemporary Italian sculptor Maurizio Cattelan open, Mary Beard and her panel discuss whether today's satirists are up to the job of lampooning contemporary politics and society. They consider whether politicians are fair game as targets for satirists, what it is like to be on the receiving end of cutting and personal satire and whether it is true that it's almost impossible to satirise the current turbulent political climate(n)

programme.title: My Brother the Devil (2012)
programme.desc: Mo, the teenage son of an Egyptian immigrant to London, idolises his older brother Rashid, a drug dealer, and longs to follow in his footsteps. However, Rashid wants Mo to make something more of his life, while realisations about himself make him question his place in the criminal underworld. Drama, starring James Floyd, Fady Elsayed and Said Taghmaoui(n)

programme.title: The Chefs' Brigade
programme.desc: Jason Atherton takes his team to the Alpine region of the Savoie in France, where Michelin star chef Alain Perillat-Mercerot has prepared a seven-course menu to test their skills. One member of the brigade decides to leave, forcing the others to tackle the challenge short-handed. Jason teaches them how to make Quenelle de Brochet, using pike caught from a nearby lake(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Ali-A's Superchargers
programme.desc: A car gets a new look inspired by planes(n)

programme.title: Top Class
programme.desc: Two teams of CBBC stars battle it out(n)

programme.title: Mustangs FC
programme.desc: Marnie is forced to play friendship umpire(n)

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.desc: Radio 1's Maya Jama is in charge as Guest Editor and we launch a brand new Blue Peter badge. Find out if Lindsey has got what it takes to conduct a world class orchestra, hear Paul McCartney's musical memories, and there's a festival in the studio with special musical guests(n)

programme.title: Super Senses: The Secret Power of Animals
programme.desc: The concluding episode explores the world of scent and examines the animals that have pushed their sense of smell far beyond human capabilities. In the Bahamas, physicist Helen Czerski dives into shark-infested waters with only a small pouch of liquid as her defence against them, while biologist Patrick Aryee controls the behaviour of a swarm of bees by using tiny traces of scent, and gets uncomfortably close to a skunk(n)

programme.title: Hairy Bikers: Route 66
programme.desc: The third leg of Dave Myers and Si King's trip sees them on the trail of Oklahoma's famous beef. In Oklahoma City, a classic T-bone steak is followed by a visit to the city's Vietnamese community, where they knock up their national dish pho - a broth made of beef or chicken. After checking out El Reno's unique onion burger the duo hook up with a couple of fire crews who like to cook - especially brisket. An organic sweet potato farm inspires Dave and Si to make sweet potato brownies, before they finish this stretch on a cattle ranch with America's most iconic custodian of the land - the cowboy(n)

programme.title: Robot Wars
programme.desc: Dara Ó Briain and Angela Scanlon present the grand final, which features the biggest ever Robot Wars battle. One of the six places in the last round remains free, and to decide who gets it, all the contestants who came second or third in the past five episodes return to battle to the death. The winner in this Ten Robot Rumble will have to face the five episode winners through a series of group battles and knockout rounds before the final showdown to decide which machine will be crowned series champion(n)

programme.title: The Wonder of Animals
programme.desc: Wildlife documentary making use of scientific research into animal anatomy to explain how various species are uniquely built to thrive in specific habitats. Chris Packham begins by examine details the penguin, detailing how the aquatic, flightless bird's legs, wings and body shape have allowed it to conquer a wide range of landscapes, from deep forests and tropical waters to bustling cities and the toughest place on the planet - Antarctica(n)

programme.title: Live Cycling
programme.desc: Clare Balding presents live coverage of the women's race, as the riders negotiate a hilly 149km course that starts in Bradford and finished in Harrogate, taking in the Yorkshire Dales en route. Great Britain's Lizzie Deignan is aiming to emulate her 2015 triumph in Richmond, USA, having returned to the sport following the birth of her first child, but she is expected to face stiff competition from reigning champion Anna van der Breggen and her Dutch compatriot Annemiek van Vleuten. With commentary by Simon Brotherton, Chris Boardman and Rochelle Gilmore(n)

programme.title: Live Athletics: World Championships
programme.desc: Gabby Logan presents further coverage of day two of the biennial event, held this year at Khalifa International Stadium in Doha, Qatar, including the men's 100m final. Justin Gatlin is the defending champion, having produced an upset in Usain Bolt's last-ever race, with the Jamaican only managing bronze, as Gatlin's American compatriot Christian Coleman took silver. Medals are also decided in the women's hammer and 10,000m and the men's long jump, and British medal hope Lynsey Sharp is expected to be involved in the semi-finals of the women's 800m. With analysis from Michael Johnson(n)

programme.title: Dad's Army
programme.desc: The older members of the platoon are threatened with being made air-raid wardens, so they take drastic action in a bid to appear younger - with Mainwaring even wearing a toupee. Comedy first aired in 1972, starring Arthur Lowe, John Le Mesurier and Clive Dunn(n)

programme.title: Marianne and Leonard: Words of Love (2019)
programme.desc: Documentary film-maker Nick Broomfield charts the relationship between Leonard Cohen and his Norwegian muse Marianne Ihlen, who met on the Greek island of Hydra in 1960 as part of a bohemian community of artists, writers and musicians. The film follows their relationship from the early days on Hydra, a humble time of 'free love' and open marriage, and reveals how their love evolved when Leonard became a successful musician(n)

programme.title: Annie Hall (1977)
programme.desc: A neurotic and self-obsessed New York Jewish comedian looks back on his affair with a scatterbrained singer, from their first encounter to the seemingly inevitable breakdown of communication, and tries to work out what went wrong. Oscar-winning romantic comedy, directed by and starring Woody Allen, with Diane Keaton, Shelley Duvall, Paul Simon, Christopher Walken and Carol Kane(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days

programme.title: Nolan: Australia's Maverick Artist
programme.desc: Profile of painter Sidney Nolan, exploring his humble origins before becoming an innovative member of the modernist movement, creating passionate depictions of his home country's iconography. The programme explores his travels round the world and how he eventually became part of a London-based community of artists, and considers how his experimental work may have inspired future generations(n)

programme.title: Black Music Legends of the 1980s
programme.desc: The life and career of the pop star, from her first taste of fame on US sitcom Diff'rent Strokes to a recording career that has seen her sell more than 65 million albums worldwide. Janet discusses the difficulties she has faced in controlling her creative and personal life, including her troubled relationship with father Joe and her collaborations with influential R&B producers Jimmy Jam and Terry Lewis. Featuring contributions by Jackie Jackson, Deborah Allen and Estelle(n)

programme.title: Joan Armatrading: Me, Myself, I
programme.desc: Known for her reclusiveness and barriers, the singer has for the first time granted access to her life and music. In this film she recounts her story from Caribbean emigre to being one of the most revered songwriters of her generation. The film covers Joan's childhood in Birmingham, her involvement in the musical Hair in 1968, her musical partnership with songwriter Pam Nestor and the relationship later forged with rock producer Glyn Johns. Plus, how she diversified her style in the 1980s, experimenting with synths and finding a new direction to create music without a producer(n)

programme.desc: Ashley Kendall, Aimee Campbell-Nottage, Donna Mullings and Simon Herdman present a poetry programme using a mix of sign language, the spoken word, music and animation to bring poems to life and capture the imaginations of both deaf and hearing children(n)

programme.title: Treasure Champs
programme.desc: Barry hogs all the toys, leaving Kari nothing to play with but a small pebble. However, when Kari turns it into a super toy character, she shows generosity by sharing it with Barry(n)

programme.title: Woolly & Tig
programme.desc: Woolly tells Tig to help a little boy on his first day at nursery school(n)

programme.title: Apple Tree House
programme.desc: Bella and the other children become transfixed on a beautiful rainbow that forms over the estate(n)

programme.title: Molly and Mack
programme.desc: Mack tries to help Daisy by bringing Twinkle the pony to the vet's surgery. Twinkle escapes and ends up in the Big Hub - which doesn't please Moira at all(n)

programme.title: Teacup Travels
programme.desc: Lokesh meets a young woman named Sakiko who is eager to impress her new mother-in-law with a tea ceremony(n)

programme.title: Sarah & Duck
programme.desc: Sarah is inspired by listening to a poetry record and decides to make up some poetry of her own(n)

programme.title: Charlie and Lola
programme.desc: Lotta does a swap with Lola, exchanging her new coat for a handbag(n)

programme.title: Tee and Mo
programme.desc: Mo can make Tee crack-up laughing with one tickle, but Tee is unable to get her to laugh in return. Tee gets more and more frustrated as he tries to find Mo's tickle spot(n)

programme.title: Clangers
programme.desc: As the air grows cold, a crystal-shaped seed lands on the Clangers' planet. Granny helps Tiny to plant it and it quickly grows into a crystal tree. Other seeds fall and soon there is a small forest. Narrated by Michael Palin(n)

programme.title: In the Night Garden
programme.desc: The Tombliboos' music makes everyone do things too fast. Igglepiggle and Upsy Daisy dance, while Makka Pakka washes stones - but when the music slows, so do they(n)

programme.title: CBeebies Bedtime Stories
programme.desc: A tale is read for younger viewers before they go to bed(n)

programme.title: EastEnders

Stuart goes to drastic lengths to help Rainie who is struggling with withdrawal symptoms. Iqra's treatment by Gray and Ian prompts her to quit her job at the restaurant. Wanda gives Ted another chance and the pair have a heart-to-heart about Joyce(n)

programme.title: Watchdog
programme.desc: Steph McGovern, Matt Allwright and Nikki Fox compare cost and customer service at the nation's biggest supermarkets, and a new Swab Mob investigation reveals hygiene failings at an iconic British institution. An airport parking company taking its customers for a ride gets a surprise of its own in Rogue Traders and Helen Skelton exposes the household staple causing chaos because of the way the packet says we should get rid of it(n)

programme.title: The Cameron Years
programme.desc: The second of the two-part documentary begins in 2005 at a moment of crisis for the Conservative Party, following three successive general election defeats. David Cameron, a relatively new MP, and his small group of supporters sought to radically shift the party's direction, taking on veteran opponent David Davis in a leadership election. Here, Cameron re-examines his desire to reshape the party - and how he risked alienating the powerful old guard as he set out to prioritise issues such as environmentalism, social justice and international aid(n)

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate from Cardiff, inviting the studio audience to question a panel of politicians and other guests on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week(n)

programme.title: Brexitcast
programme.desc: The TV version of the popular radio show taking a light-hearted view of all things related to Brexit and beyond, with Laura Kuenssberg, Katya Adler, Adam Fleming and Chris Mason engaging in banter about the ongoing process to leave the EU(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Jon Kay and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Crimewatch Roadshow
programme.desc: Michelle Ackerley heads to south Wales to discover how police forces bring different teams together to train for the most challenging situations. Back in the studio, Rav Wilding has the latest crime appeals from all over the UK, as well as tips, advice and features(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer

programme.title: Defenders UK
programme.desc: The enforcement officers battle to keep the roads safe after a lorry driver killed four people as a result of using his mobile phone while driving. They also crack down on the rogue airport car parking companies ripping off holiday-makers(n)

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: Nick Knowles examines the extraordinary rescue of a young man missing overnight after being swept out to sea on his surfboard. Plus, a local resident comes to the aid of a young woman who topples into a city-centre river during a night on the town with friends(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Eric Knowles hosts as experts Nick Hall and Richard Madley help two teams scour Exeter's Westpoint Arena for items. The presenter also receives a visit by the chairman of the Torquay Pottery Collectors Society, who has a rather special pasty to show him(n)

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Al has bonded with Ray, but Eve is about to discover something to upset them all. Sid offers support to a friend of his father who is struggling with getting older(n)

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Seventeen contenders start this edition of the quiz, but how many of them will survive to face the £10,000 Question? Hosted by Rick Edwards(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich are tasked to create a garden that fuses Japan and Yorkshire - in Cheshunt, Hertfordshire. Yorkshire-born James wants to see hints of his native county in his new Home Counties house, while his Japanese wife Akika, who he met when traveling, naturally wants to feel a link to her upbringing and homeland(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Arusha Irvine enjoys the fun of the fair, while Angus Ashworth heads to Plymouth to discover the turbulent history of gin, before having a go as a Master Distiller. They then join up at their final auction in Clevedon, north Somerset(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: Strictly: It Takes Two
programme.desc: Zoe Ball and Rylan Clark-Neal present analysis of the pro-celebrity couples' progress ahead of tomorrow night's show, and reports from the rehearsals. Celebrity super fans discuss all things ballroom, while Gethin Jones catches up with the couples backstage at this week's Strictly rehearsals(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: The usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances(n)

programme.title: Iolo's Street Life
programme.desc: Iolo Williams finds birds and bees are active on the streets of Colwyn Bay. Robins, sparrows, tits, wagtails and pigeons are nesting in drain pipes, road signs, wall holes, security cages and, in Brecon, even on a traffic light. In the south Wales valleys' town of Deri, what can only be described as a nature reserve has been created in the back garden of a terraced house, attracting numerous hedgehogs to feast(n)

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: A World Athletics Championships edition of the quiz, which sees former Great Britain stars Denise Lewis, Liz McColgan, Greg Rutherford and Kriss Akabusi join host Sue Barker and team captains Phil Tufnell and Matt Dawson on the quiz(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Callum shares his concerns about Rainie to Stuart but his mind turns elsewhere when Whitney returns to collect the rest of her belongings - and is taken aback when she drop a bombshell. Sharon is left fearing the worst, and Ben asks an old friend for help with his new money-making scheme(n)

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: Contenders battle for the remaining places in the semi-finals, tasked to cook a faultless two-course meal that will not only be judged by John Torode and Gregg Wallace but also by former contenders Alexis Conran, Sophie Thompson and the Rev Richard Coles. All three know what it takes to get through this competition and are in for a treat as the four celebs push the boat out to extend their stay in the contest(n)

programme.title: Scarborough
programme.desc: After a bit of gentle persuasion by Marion, Mike and Karen decide to move in together and go flat-hunting in the town. However, Mike's promise to Karen that he'll tell Bigsy that he's moving out is proving more difficult to deliver than he thought. Bigsy has other things on his mind though, namely Lisa Marie. Meanwhile, Geraldine finally admits to Karen that she's feeling the strain of the salon's financial difficulties and things only get worse when the police pay her a visit. Comedy, starring Maggie Ollerenshaw, Catherine Tyldesley and Jason Manford(n)

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host greets Oscar-winning actress Helen Mirren, who is starring in the new TV drama Catherine the Great. Author, adventurer and documentary-maker Simon Reeve also appears, as does comedian and actor Jack Whitehall, who stood in as host for Graham during the spring. Plus, the one and only Rupaul discusses bringing his hit show to the BBC in RuPaul's Drag Race UK. Music is supplied courtesy of Normani, who performs her new single Motivation(n)

programme.title: Lewis Capaldi Live
programme.desc: The breakthrough pop sensation performs the biggest headline show of his career at Croxteth Park in Liverpool, entertaining a sell-out crowd of 12,500 people. Accompanied by the 60-piece Manchester Camerata, Lewis delights the audience with hits from his platinum-selling debut album and a special surprise cover of a Beatles classic(n)

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Back in 1983, 16-year-old Amy Taskerland was murdered on the night of the school disco. More than 30 years later, the unearthing of a time capsule and the discovery of her voice on a mix tape inside prompts Miller and co to delve back into the case, starting by interviewing Amy's best friend Harriet and her former teacher Mr Hines. The recording features a strange mix of music and poetry, including words from a speech written in secret for the Queen for broadcast in the event of a nuclear war. But how did Amy get a copy of it, and was its secret nature the reason she was killed? Elsewhere, Miller enjoys her first romance since her divorce, while Gerry goes on his future son-in-law's stag night. Amy Nuttall (Emmerdale, Downton Abbey) and Jack Shepherd (Wycliffe) guest star(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Jon Kay and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45(n)

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Ching-He Huang and Cyrus Todiwala, and special guest Alexander Armstrong. Plus, archive clips featuring Rick Stein, Keith Floyd, and Nigella Lawson(n)

programme.title: Down the Social with Owen Money
programme.desc: Comedian Owen Money and co-presenter Mandy Starr visit the Resolven Miners Welfare, which is in crisis. They meet devoted members fighting to save the club and discover a secret, vast theatre within the club, hidden away and unseen for almost 20 years. Owen vows to help those members fighting to save this precious building from closing for ever(n)

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests look ahead to the weekend's football, including Everton v Manchester City. Plus, updates on the lunchtime game between Sheffield United and Liverpool(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion Dublin visits a bungalow in Cheshire and an old chapel in Dudley ripe for development into flats. Martel Maxwell is in West Yorkshire, where the new owners of an end of terrace have grand plans for a child-friendly development(n)

programme.title: Live Athletics: World Championships
programme.desc: Gabby Logan presents coverage of day two of the biennial event, held this year at Khalifa International Stadium in Doha, Qatar. Great Britain's Dina Asher-Smith begins her bid for a sprint double with the heats of the 100m, while other events include the heats of the men's 800m and qualifying for the men's discus and pole vault. With analysis from Michael Johnson(n)

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents a round-up of the classified results and this afternoon's news from the Premier League, EFL and Scotland(n)

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman invite another selection of famous faces to take part in the unorthodox general knowledge quiz. As always, the challengers are tasked with coming up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public. This time, all the contestants come from the world of music, as Tom Ogden, Ray BLK, Shaun Ryder, Rowetta, Ricky Wilson, Will Young, Naughty Boy and Lesley Garrett take part. Plus, a special performance from the band Blossoms(n)

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman present the first live episode of this year's series, with a line-up featuring the likes of Alex Scott, Emma Barton and James Cracknell. The celebrities are dancing to impress the four judges and the viewers at home, who get the chance to vote for their favourites for the very first time this series(n)

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Hospital drama with the staff of Holby's accident and emergency department as they deal with more sick and injured patients. David and Ollie continue to clash after Ollie's operation, and David is taken aback when he finds out that Ollie has invited Rosa round to keep him company. Meanwhile, Rash decides to fight to stay in Holby, and new paramedic Lev arrives late for work after saving some kittens. Starring Jason Durr, Harry Collett, Jacey Salles, Neet Mohan and Uriel Emil(n)

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: Cathy seems reluctant to reveal any information about her new boyfriend, Mark refuses to talk about why his wife has thrown him out, and Dermot lies to his fiancee's parents about his job - all of which makes Agnes feel her family is becoming too secretive. Comedy, written by and starring Brendan O'Carroll(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Sheffield United v Liverpool at Bramall Lane and Everton v Manchester City at Goodison Park, as both of last season's top two faced potentially difficult away games. Plus, Aston Villa v Burnley, Bournemouth v West Ham United, Chelsea v Brighton & Hove Albion, Crystal Palace v Norwich City, Tottenham Hotspur v Southampton and Wolverhampton Wanderers v Watford(n)

programme.title: A View from the Terrace
programme.desc: Craig G Telfer, Craig Fowler, Shaughan McGuigan and Joel Sked present a sideways look at Scottish football, dissecting of ins and outs of the week's action, with a variety of features covering the full spectrum from the giants to the minnows(n)

programme.title: Stupid Man, Smart Phone
programme.desc: Russell Kane and Arron Crascall go on adventures around the world, using only a smartphone to gain the vital information they need to survive. In the first episode, they cross the Sahara, where they face challenges including making a fire with camel dung, cooking a lizard while it is still alive, and surviving an encounter with a spitting cobra(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Jon Kay and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45(n)

programme.title: Mary Berry's Quick Cooking
programme.desc: Mary heads into London's West End for inspiration, seeking out the secrets of getting busy people fed quickly and well. She learns the art of high-speed wok cooking in Chinatown and sampling the sweet treats being made daily at London's first-ever patisserie, before seeing a show and having a late-night cocktail with some of the cast members. Back in her own kitchen, Mary creates an Asian beef and pepper stir fry, a chocolate cappuccino pie and an easy, speedy breakfast for those on the go(n)

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests look ahead to the weekend's football, including Everton v Manchester City. Plus, updates on the lunchtime game between Sheffield United and Liverpool(n)

Meanwhile, Rash decides to fight to stay in Holby, and new paramedic Lev arrives late for work after saving some kittens. Starring Jason Durr, Harry Collett, Jacey Salles, Neet Mohan and Uriel Emil(n)

Comedy, written by and starring Brendan O'Carroll(n)

programme.desc: Roving reporter Michelle Ackerley heads to Bristol to find out how money and assets seized from convicted criminals are being ploughed back into the community. The programme also heads to an auction where the proceeds of crime are sold off to raise those funds(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The development progress of semi-detached properties in Sneyd Green in Stoke on Trent and Low Hill in Wolverhampton, as well as a piece of land in Leyland, Lancashire(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: A special edition for BBC Music Day, which sees the Darkness frontman Justin Hawkins and bass guitarist Frankie Poullain competing against Feeder singer Grant Nicholas and bassist Taka Hirose. They'll be scouring an antiques fair in Stamford Meadows, Lincolnshire, with the help of Kate Bliss and Philip Serrell, in a bid to make the most cash - and while they're busy, presenter Natasha Raskin Sharp pays a visit to the BBC's famous Maida Vale studios(n)

programme.desc: Ayesha intervenes to prevent Al from destroying his mum's happiness. Zara is shaken by some news from Daniel. Valerie becomes entangled in her hairstylist's relationship(n)

programme.title: Impossible

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: The Rich Brothers and Charlie Dimmock compete for the chance to turn a Goring-by-Sea garden into an Australia-themed bolt-hole, with water feature, fire bowl and palm trees(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Arusha Irvine and Angus Ashworth tour the West Country, where Roo meets Exmoor's equine inhabitants and Angus bares all at an exclusive beach that can only be accessed by tunnel(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: Strictly: It Takes Two

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Mel continues to blackmail Sharon, leaving Lisa to question what exactly is going on - but Mel refuses to reveal the truth. Stuart goes to drastic lengths to help Rainie who is struggling with withdrawal symptoms. Iqra's treatment by Gray and Ian prompts her to quit her job at the restaurant. Wanda gives Ted another chance and the pair have a heart-to-heart about Joyce(n)

programme.title: Watchdog
programme.desc: Steph McGovern, Matt Allwright and Nikki Fox compare cost and customer service at the nation's biggest supermarkets, and a new Swab Mob investigation reveals hygiene failings at an iconic British institution. An airport parking company taking its customers for a ride gets a surprise of its own in Rogue Traders and Helen Skelton exposes the household staple causing chaos because of the way the packet says we should get rid of it(n)

programme.title: The Cameron Years
programme.desc: The second of the two-part documentary begins in 2005 at a moment of crisis for the Conservative Party, following three successive general election defeats. David Cameron, a relatively new MP, and his small group of supporters sought to radically shift the party's direction, taking on veteran opponent David Davis in a leadership election. Here, Cameron re-examines his desire to reshape the party - and how he risked alienating the powerful old guard as he set out to prioritise issues such as environmentalism, social justice and international aid(n)

programme.title: The View
programme.desc: Mark Carruthers presents a review of the week's political news, including reports on the latest events at Stormont and Westminster. Plus, comment and analysis with studio guests(n)

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate from Cardiff, inviting the studio audience to question a panel of politicians and other guests on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week(n)

programme.title: Brexitcast
programme.desc: The TV version of the popular radio show taking a light-hearted view of all things related to Brexit and beyond, with Laura Kuenssberg, Katya Adler, Adam Fleming and Chris Mason engaging in banter about the ongoing process to leave the EU(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Jon Kay and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Crimewatch Roadshow
programme.desc: Michelle Ackerley heads to south Wales to discover how police forces bring different teams together to train for the most challenging situations. Back in the studio, Rav Wilding has the latest crime appeals from all over the UK, as well as tips, advice and features(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell explore a couple of terraces in Liverpool and Alfreton in Derbyshire, as well as a rambling former pub in Rowelton, Cumbria(n)

programme.title: Defenders UK
programme.desc: The enforcement officers battle to keep the roads safe after a lorry driver killed four people as a result of using his mobile phone while driving. They also crack down on the rogue airport car parking companies ripping off holiday-makers(n)

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: Nick Knowles examines the extraordinary rescue of a young man missing overnight after being swept out to sea on his surfboard. Plus, a local resident comes to the aid of a young woman who topples into a city-centre river during a night on the town with friends(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Eric Knowles hosts as experts Nick Hall and Richard Madley help two teams scour Exeter's Westpoint Arena for items. The presenter also receives a visit by the chairman of the Torquay Pottery Collectors Society, who has a rather special pasty to show him(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Al has bonded with Ray, but Eve is about to discover something to upset them all. Sid offers support to a friend of his father who is struggling with getting older(n)

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Seventeen contenders start this edition of the quiz, but how many of them will survive to face the £10,000 Question? Hosted by Rick Edwards(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich are tasked to create a garden that fuses Japan and Yorkshire - in Cheshunt, Hertfordshire. Yorkshire-born James wants to see hints of his native county in his new Home Counties house, while his Japanese wife Akika, who he met when traveling, naturally wants to feel a link to her upbringing and homeland(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Arusha Irvine enjoys the fun of the fair, while Angus Ashworth heads to Plymouth to discover the turbulent history of gin, before having a go as a Master Distiller. They then join up at their final auction in Clevedon, north Somerset(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.desc: Zoe Ball and Rylan Clark-Neal present analysis of the pro-celebrity couples' progress ahead of tomorrow night's show, and reports from the rehearsals. Celebrity super fans discuss all things ballroom, while Gethin Jones catches up with the couples backstage at this week's Strictly rehearsals(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: The usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and green-fingered siblings Harry and David Rich travel to Milton Keynes to compete for the chance to create a garden for husband and wife Vinay and Raunak. Vinay loves to host barbeques, so he's after a garden where he can do all that in style. Raunak, meanwhile, is more interested in filling the garden with vivid and dramatic colours, to match the style of decor she has used throughout their house. Both want the garden to represent the Hindu religion's belief in the power and importance of nature(n)

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: A World Athletics Championships edition of the quiz, which sees former Great Britain stars Denise Lewis, Liz McColgan, Greg Rutherford and Kriss Akabusi join host Sue Barker and team captains Phil Tufnell and Matt Dawson on the quiz(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Callum shares his concerns about Rainie to Stuart but his mind turns elsewhere when Whitney returns to collect the rest of her belongings - and is taken aback when she drop a bombshell. Sharon is left fearing the worst, and Ben asks an old friend for help with his new money-making scheme(n)

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: Contenders battle for the remaining places in the semi-finals, tasked to cook a faultless two-course meal that will not only be judged by John Torode and Gregg Wallace but also by former contenders Alexis Conran, Sophie Thompson and the Rev Richard Coles. All three know what it takes to get through this competition and are in for a treat as the four celebs push the boat out to extend their stay in the contest(n)

programme.title: Scarborough
programme.desc: After a bit of gentle persuasion by Marion, Mike and Karen decide to move in together and go flat-hunting in the town. However, Mike's promise to Karen that he'll tell Bigsy that he's moving out is proving more difficult to deliver than he thought. Bigsy has other things on his mind though, namely Lisa Marie. Meanwhile, Geraldine finally admits to Karen that she's feeling the strain of the salon's financial difficulties and things only get worse when the police pay her a visit. Comedy, starring Maggie Ollerenshaw, Catherine Tyldesley and Jason Manford(n)

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host greets Oscar-winning actress Helen Mirren, who is starring in the new TV drama Catherine the Great. Author, adventurer and documentary-maker Simon Reeve also appears, as does comedian and actor Jack Whitehall, who stood in as host for Graham during the spring. Plus, the one and only Rupaul discusses bringing his hit show to the BBC in RuPaul's Drag Race UK. Music is supplied courtesy of Normani, who performs her new single Motivation(n)

programme.title: Lewis Capaldi Live
programme.desc: The breakthrough pop sensation performs the biggest headline show of his career at Croxteth Park in Liverpool, entertaining a sell-out crowd of 12,500 people. Accompanied by the 60-piece Manchester Camerata, Lewis delights the audience with hits from his platinum-selling debut album and a special surprise cover of a Beatles classic(n)

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Back in 1983, 16-year-old Amy Taskerland was murdered on the night of the school disco. More than 30 years later, the unearthing of a time capsule and the discovery of her voice on a mix tape inside prompts Miller and co to delve back into the case, starting by interviewing Amy's best friend Harriet and her former teacher Mr Hines. The recording features a strange mix of music and poetry, including words from a speech written in secret for the Queen for broadcast in the event of a nuclear war. But how did Amy get a copy of it, and was its secret nature the reason she was killed? Elsewhere, Miller enjoys her first romance since her divorce, while Gerry goes on his future son-in-law's stag night. Amy Nuttall (Emmerdale, Downton Abbey) and Jack Shepherd (Wycliffe) guest star(n)

programme.title: RWC 2019 Live
programme.desc: England v USA (Kick-off 11.45am). Coverage of the Pool C clash, which takes place at Kobe Misaki Stadium in Kobe City, Japan. The last time these nations faced each other at a World Cup was in France in 2007, and on that occasion England triumphed by a score of 28-10, in what was considered a lacklustre performance. Pundits at the time commented that the side was a shadow of the 2003 World Cup-winning squad, and predicted them to struggle, but they would of course go on to reach the final before succumbing to South Africa. Presented by Mark Pougatch, with analysis from Jonny Wilkinson, Sir Clive Woodward and Lawrence Dallaglio, pitch-side contributions by David Flatman, and commentary from Nick Mullins, Ben Kay and Ugo Monye(n)

programme.title: James Martin's French Adventure

programme.title: Inside Prison: Britain Behind Bars
programme.desc: Documentary going inside the walls of six prisons to offer a sharp insight into the harsh realities of life behind bars. Thirty-year-old repeat offender Gemma, serving five years for burglary at HMP Downview in Surrey, talks about how her upbringing led her into a life of crime. She has a reputation for bad behaviour - which soon becomes apparent when she threatens a fellow prisoner. Meanwhile, new officer Jon Aldridge begins his first shift at HMP Bullingdon in Oxfordshire and seeks advice from one of the most respected men on the wing - top dog Anthony(n)

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: A college dean is found bludgeoned to death in local woodland, following a heated pub debate. When an alchemic image is discovered by the body, denoting the first of four stages of a process called 'The Magnum Opus', the detectives realise that there are three more murders to follow. Before any more crimes are committed, the team must uncover the guilty secret that the victims share in order to learn the identity of the killer and the potential victims(n)

programme.title: Lethal Weapon
programme.desc: Cole finds himself on the rebound after Natalie's engagement, and his new romantic interest, Julie has an unexpected connection to Assistant District Attorney Erica, complicating things at work. Meanwhile, Murtaugh worries about the connection between the murder he's investigating and a new opportunity for Trish to serve as co-counsel on a high-profile lawsuit alongside Leo Getz. Action drama, starring Damon Wayans and Seann William Scott(n)

programme.title: Take Me Out
programme.desc: Club promoter Seeb from Belfast, barman Ben from Oxford, scuba-diving instructor Will from Preston and tailor Phil from Liverpool all hope to find their valentine among a line-up of 30 single women. However, first they must get the female participants to keep their lights on as a sign of approval. Host Paddy McGuinness monitors their progress and reviews the dating action from last week's couples on the Isle of Fernando's(n)

programme.title: The Level
programme.desc: Nancy visits the latest suspect, garage owner and Le Saux associate Duncan Elliot, where she spots Theo Kettler's missing car. A tense chase ensues and after the driver escapes, Nancy and O'Dowd begin to suspect Gunner of complicity. A vital piece of evidence is later recovered from the vehicle, giving DCI Newman a breakthrough - but placing Nancy in even greater danger. Meanwhile, tensions surface among the Le Sauxs when Hayley and her brother Tate inherit the haulage yard. Karla Crome, Robert James-Collier and Lindsey Coulson star(n)

programme.title: RWC 2019 Live
programme.desc: Argentina v Tonga (Kick-off 5.45am). Coverage of the Pool C clash, which takes place at Hanazono Rugby Stadium in Higashiosaka City, Japan. Argentina have a commendable World Cup pedigree having reached the last four in 2007 and 2015, and despite a poor run of form this year will be considered favourites for victory here. Presented by Mark Pougatch, with analysis from Brian O'Driscoll, Michael Lynagh and Paul O'Connell, and commentary by Simon Ward and Danielle Waterman(n)

programme.title: RWC 2019 Live
programme.desc: Japan v Ireland (Kick-off 8.15am). Mark Pougatch presents all the action from the Pool A encounter at Tokyo Stadium. Ireland entered the tournament as favourites to win the pool, but they will not be taking the hosts lightly after they shocked the rugby world four years ago with a victory over South Africa. With analysis from Brian O'Driscoll, Sam Warburton and Paul O'Connell, reports by Jill Douglas, and commentary from Miles Harrison, Alan Quinlan and Shane Williams(n)

programme.title: RWC 2019 Live
programme.desc: South Africa v Namibia (Kick-off 10.45am). Craig Doyle presents coverage of the Pool B match at City of Toyota Stadium in Japan. The Springboks start as strong favourites against their African neighbours, but will be wary of complacency after their shock defeat at the hands of Japan four years ago in Brighton. With analysis from Maggie Alphonsi, Sam Warburton and Lawrence Dallaglio, reports by Jill Douglas and Bryan Habana, and commentary from Nick Mullins, Ben Kay and Ugo Monye(n)

programme.title: RWC 2019 Live
programme.desc: England v USA (Kick-off 11.45am). Coverage of the Pool C clash, which takes place at Kobe Misaki Stadium in Kobe City, Japan. The last time these nations faced each other at a World Cup was in France in 2007, and on that occasion England triumphed by a score of 28-10, in what was considered a lacklustre performance. Pundits at the time commented that the side was a shadow of the 2003 World Cup-winning squad, and predicted them to struggle, but they would of course go on to reach the final before succumbing to South Africa. Presented by Mark Pougatch, with analysis from Jonny Wilkinson, Sir Clive Woodward and Lawrence Dallaglio, pitch-side contributions by David Flatman, and commentary from Nick Mullins, Ben Kay and Ugo Monye(n)

The heroic copper doggedly pursues his quarry to the detriment of his relationship with Tricia - but tragedy strikes, leaving him heartbroken(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Michael is getting worried about his trial. Kim discovers her credit card has been cancelled. Carol receives a phone call from Lorraine's headmistress(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Frank tells Chris that he is divorcing Kim. Mrs Jeffries suggests Carol speak to a social worker about Lorraine. Zoe tells Archie she has got a job interview in Edinburgh(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Tracy gets four GCSEs, but Ken is disappointed. Kevin is worried about the trial. Angie tells Curly she's trapped and wants out. Sally tells Gail about Joe's declaration(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Curly agrees to be Reg's best man in the hope Angie will get romantic at the wedding. Amy is thrilled that Andy likes Dominic so much. Des talks to Angie about what she wants(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A walk in the woods leads to tragedy for Emily Simpson, who witnesses something unsettling before being found dead in her cottage by old friend Lucy Bellringer. Convinced natural causes are not to blame, Lucy calls in DCI Barnaby and Sergeant Troy, whose investigation eventually uncovers sinister long-buried secrets. The first ever episode of the long-running detective drama, guest starring Jessica Hynes and Emily Mortimer(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Mike joins forces with Scotland Yard when a gang of thieves turns up in Aidensfield, apparently plotting to steal a valuable horse from local trainer Kieron Doyle. The heroic copper doggedly pursues his quarry to the detriment of his relationship with Tricia - but tragedy strikes, leaving him heartbroken(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A woman's body is discovered in Captain Hastings' garage on Bonfire Night. It transpires that the deceased was due to marry an MP - and although the evidence suggests suicide, the Belgian detective suspects foul play. David Suchet, Hugh Fraser and Philip Jackson star(n)

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: The detective investigates the discovery of an unidentified teenage boy's body in a coastal town's shipyard, who is revealed to be listed as a missing person, having absconded from the care of his support worker. Vera discovers that the quiet community where he was found is plagued by a serious drugs problem - despite the fact that the only known dealer in the area has turned his back on crime. The victim could be the answer to the mystery, but a wall of silence surrounds his death. Mark Addy guest stars(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: When police sergeant Pete Garvey is killed, the investigation proves harrowing for Devlin as he was one of his best friends. The detective discovers the officer had recently been in contact with their childhood parish priest Jonathan Nugent, who Pete claimed sexually abused him as a child. Unable to place a straightforward murder charge against Nugent, Steel and Phillips take an unusual approach to bring him to trial. Guest starring Matthew Marsh, Ruth Gemmell and Patrick Malahide, with Jamie Bamber(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: While investigating the murder of prison officer Charlie Tyner, Brooks and Devlin discover he had been abusing his position and convicts in his charge. A suspect is brought to trial, but Steel and Phillips go in pursuit of a career criminal connected to Tyner and have to persuade their key witness to give evidence against a man she claims to love. Starring Bradley Walsh, Jamie Bamber, Ben Daniels and Freema Agyeman(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: Leaving behind his beloved Oxford, Morse and a reluctant Lewis set off to investigate the bizarre death of an Englishwoman who was attending a psychotherapy clinic in northern Italy. Their inquiries lead them to the vineyards and villas of Vicenza and the city of Verona - as well as a glamorous opera singer and a convicted fraudster. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star as the detective duo, with Frances Barber, Michael Kitchen, James Grout and Jolyon Baker(n)
